title: Evidence Foundry Unsigned Release Manifest
description: Validates build/kb-pack/<moduleId>/<packVersion>/release-manifest.unsigned.json (evidence-foundry-buildout P5-T1, FR-18, `02 §4.18` MINUS the `signature` block) AND the same document once P3-T1/T2's `tools/release-sign/` tooling (evidence-foundry-e1, not yet built) has run over it. It binds exactly the fields this task's own acceptance criteria names -- `rfInputs[].{runId, bundleSha256, claimLedgerSha256, verificationExitCode}`, `converter.{name, version, configSha256}`, `testCorpusHash`, `traceabilityHash` -- plus (P1-T5, ADR-0005, FR-14/FR-16) an OPTIONAL `dryRun`/`signature` pair that stays absent on the E0 unsigned stub this task's converter emits today and is schema-forced-empty on any real (non-dry-run) candidate once `tools/release-sign` exists -- and rejects everything else via `additionalProperties: false`. `02 §4.18`'s full production manifest ALSO carries `knowledgeBaseVersion`, `clinicalContentHash`, `approvedBy[]`, `validationRunId`, `releasedAt`; none of those belong on THIS document, because none of them are true yet for a staged, unsigned, unreviewed proposal (CLAUDE.md: no artifact is described as clinically validated or release-ready before the real validation/signing gates run) -- inventing those fields here would misrepresent an unsigned stub as release-ready. `signature` is the sole exception, and only because P1-T5's own acceptance criteria requires the slot to exist NOW, forced structurally empty, so no later task can introduce it un-gated (mirrors `schemas/module-manifest.schema.json`'s `approvedBy` / `schemas/rule.schema.json`'s `clinicalApprovers` `maxItems: 0` idiom for an array field, using the object-typed equivalent -- `type: "null"` -- for this object-typed field, per the `$defs/signature` `if`/`then`/`else` below). A populated `signature` is legal ONLY when `dryRun` is present and `true` AND `signature.keyId` carries the structural `TESTKEY-` marker (OQ-6 ephemeral test-key convention) -- there is no schema branch under which a real (non-dry-run) candidate's `signature` can be anything but `null`. Structural validity here never implies clinical validity, safety, release authorization, or that any human signing custodian (gate G2) has acted. scripts/validate-kb.mjs wires this schema in as an EXISTENCE-GATED check across the whole build/kb-pack/ tree (gitignored/ephemeral per P1-T7): the directory usually does not exist at all in a clean checkout, and its absence -- or the absence of a given pack-version's own release-manifest.unsigned.json -- is not itself an error.

dryRun.description: OPTIONAL. ADR-0005/OQ-6's structural dry-run marker, absent on every manifest E0's converter emits today (this key does not exist in that pipeline's output, and its absence is the honest, unsigned default -- see `signature`'s own description). `tools/release-sign` (P3-T2, not yet built) sets this to `true` ONLY on ephemeral, in-memory-keypair test-key candidates it produces for its own dry-run mode; it never sets `true` on a real signing-ceremony (G2) candidate. `false` and absent are treated identically by the `allOf` conditional below -- both route to the `signature: null` branch -- so a future writer cannot launder a real candidate through an explicit `dryRun: false` past the forced-empty check.

signature.description: OPTIONAL key; REQUIRED to be `null` whenever present unless `dryRun` is also present and `true` (enforced by the top-level `allOf`/`if`/`then`/`else`, not expressible with `type` alone). Detached Ed25519 shape (`{algorithm, keyId, value}`, ADR-0005 mechanism, same object shape the OQ-2 review-record `signature` slot uses) once populated on a `dryRun: true` test-key candidate; `keyId` MUST carry the `TESTKEY-` prefix in that case (OQ-6). No real signing-custodian key (gate G2) is ever legal here -- this schema has no branch that admits one, by design, until a future, separately gated schema revision explicitly raises this ceiling.

moduleId.description: Must equal the name of the <moduleId> directory this manifest was found under (build/kb-pack/<moduleId>/<packVersion>/) -- this schema validates the document in isolation and cannot cross-check its own filesystem location; scripts/validate-kb.mjs's validateReleaseManifest() enforces that agreement.

packVersion.description: Must equal the name of the <packVersion> directory this manifest was found under -- same cross-check caveat as moduleId above.

rfInputs.description: Every verified `rf` bundle this staged pack proposal was drafted from (`02 §4.18`'s own `rfInputs[]` array). Exactly the 4 fields this task's acceptance criteria binds -- `runId`, `bundleSha256`, `claimLedgerSha256`, `verificationExitCode` -- no `bundleId` (the full production manifest example carries one; this unsigned E0 manifest deliberately does not, per the binding task-row scope).

testCorpusHash.description: SHA-256 over this module's generated engine-test corpus (tests/ef-<moduleId>-*.test.mjs, P4-T5..T8), name + content, sorted by filename so the digest does not depend on directory-listing order.

traceabilityHash.description: SHA-256 over the traceability-bearing artifacts this run has in hand (authoring-decisions.yaml, evidence-assertions.json, rule-provenance.json, rules.json), name + content, sorted by name. Pending P5-T4's dedicated source->passage->claim->decision->rule->test->output index artifact, this is the interim, honest, deterministic definition of `02 §4.18`'s `traceabilityHash` field for E0's plumbing -- not a clinical value of any kind.

runId.description: The upstream rf run id this pack proposal traces back to (`evidence_bundle.yaml.run_id`, mirrors pack-provenance.json's own `rfRunId` field, P3-T7).

bundleSha256.description: SHA-256 of the pinned evidence_bundle.yaml bytes (hashing.mjs#pinArtifacts, P2-T3) -- the same digest pack-provenance.json's own `upstreamVerification.bundleSha256` records, here with the repo-standard `sha256:` prefix.

claimLedgerSha256.description: SHA-256 of the pinned claims/claim_ledger.yaml bytes (hashing.mjs#pinArtifacts, P2-T3).

verificationExitCode.description: The upstream rf verification run's own recorded exit code (reviews/verification.yaml.exit_code), reconciled against evidence_bundle.yaml.status by eligibility.mjs's invariants 1/3/4 before this converter ever proceeds -- always 0 by the time a manifest is emitted, but recorded verbatim rather than hardcoded.

name.description: This converter's own identity ("rf-bundle-to-kb-pack"), matching pack-provenance.json's own converter.name (P3-T7).

version.description: This converter build's own version constant (CONVERTER_VERSION, propose.mjs) -- bumped only when the tool's own behavior changes in a way that could change emitted bytes for identical inputs (seam invariant 13).

configSha256.description: SHA-256 over every .mjs source file this converter build ships (tools/rf-bundle-to-kb-pack/**/*.mjs, name + content, sorted) -- an automatic, content-derived cross-check on top of the manually-bumped `version` string: any change to this tool's own code changes this hash, whether or not `version` was remembered to be bumped.

dryRunSignature.description: The ONLY legal non-null shape of `signature`, and only reachable when `dryRun: true` -- P3-T2's ephemeral test-key dry-run mode (OQ-6). Mirrors `schemas/review-record.schema.json`'s OQ-2 synthetic-signature shape byte-for-byte (same `algorithm`/`keyId`/`value` fields, same `TESTKEY-` marker convention) so both signature slots share one mental model.

algorithm.description: ADR-0005's recommended algorithm. Fixed literal -- no other value is legal, dry-run or otherwise.

keyId.description: MUST carry the structural `TESTKEY-` prefix (OQ-6) -- the schema-level proof that this key is an ephemeral, in-memory, never-written-to-disk test key, never a real signing-custodian (G2) identity. A real `keyId` cannot appear here because `dryRun: true` is itself schema-impossible on anything other than a test-key candidate under this project's own tooling contract (P3-T2); this pattern is the second, independent, schema-enforced guarantee.

value.description: Base64/hex detached-signature bytes over the canonicalized manifest bytes minus this `signature` object itself (same ADR-0005 mechanism the OQ-2 review-record signature slot uses).
